{{Quote|As long as the memory of Jabiim's former glory lives, we'll fight to restore it.|[[Nolan Gillmunn]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: In the Shadows of Their Fathers">''[[Star Wars: Empire: In the Shadows of Their Fathers]]''</ref>}}
The '''Jabiimi Loyalists''' were the faction of [[Jabiimi]]s under [[Orliss Gillmunn]] who opposed [[Alto Stratus]]'s [[Jabiimi Nationalists|Nationalist]] faction during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. After Gillmunn was killed in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], leadership passed to his son, [[Nolan Gillmunn|Nolan]].

Their rebellion attracted the attention of such potential offworld patrons as the [[Kuati/Legends|Kuati]], but the Loyalists were left with a bitter aftertaste of their betrayal from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and vowed to liberate themselves on their own. After the Clone Wars, the Loyalists continued to oppose the Nationalists, now allied with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

Finally, [[0 ABY/Legends|eight months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Gillmunn allied with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], reasoning that while they could defeat the Nationalists on their own, they would need outside help to fend off the Empire. The [[Subjugation of Jabiim|ensuing battle]], however, was a debacle that resulted in the mass enslavement of Jabiim's population.
